crossref.abstract: <jats:title>Abstract</jats:title><jats:p>Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup>+N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub> → Ar+N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub><jats:sup>+</jats:sup> has served as a paradigm for charge-transfer dynamics studies during the last several decades. Despite significant experimental and theoretical efforts on this model system, state-resolved experimental investigations on the microscopic charge-transfer mechanism between the spin-orbit excited Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup>(<jats:sup>2</jats:sup>P<jats:sub>1/2</jats:sub>) ion and N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub> have been rare. Here, we measure the first quantum state-to-state differential cross sections for Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup>+N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub> → Ar+N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub><jats:sup>+</jats:sup> with the Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup> ion prepared exclusively in the spin-orbit excited state <jats:sup>2</jats:sup>P<jats:sub>1/2</jats:sub> on a crossed-beam setup with three-dimensional velocity-map imaging. Trajectory surface-hopping calculations qualitatively reproduce the vibrationally dependent rotational and angular distributions of the N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub><jats:sup>+</jats:sup> product. Both the scattering images and theoretical calculations show that the charge-transfer dynamics of the spin-orbit excited Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup>(<jats:sup>2</jats:sup>P<jats:sub>1/2</jats:sub>) ion differs significantly from that of the spin-orbit ground Ar<jats:sup>+</jats:sup>(<jats:sup>2</jats:sup>P<jats:sub>3/2</jats:sub>) when colliding with N<jats:sub>2</jats:sub>. Such state-to-state information makes quantitative understanding of this benchmark charge-transfer reaction within reach.</jats:p>
title: Imaging the state-to-state charge-transfer dynamics between the spin-orbit excited Ar+(2P1/2) ion and N2
